title: Use of os.system()

reasoning: os.system() is deprecated and vulnerable to command injection.

title: Potential hardcoded secret value
description: Potential hardcoded credential or secret detected.
reasoning: Potential hardcoded credential or secret detected.

title: Division by Zero in feature engineering
description: Division by zero risk when total_available is zero in the need_to_available_ratio calculation.
code_snippet: float(total_need / total_available) if total_available > 0 else float(total_need)
reasoning: While there is a conditional check, it is better practice to handle potential floating point precision issues or ensure total_available is checked robustly before any division.
suggested_fix: Ensure total_available is checked against a small epsilon rather than just 0 if dealing with floating point types.

title: Broad exception clause with pass
description: Catching exceptions and passing silently can hide critical bugs.
reasoning: Catching exceptions and passing silently can hide critical bugs.

title: Unsafe dictionary access
description: Accessing dictionary keys without checking existence can cause a KeyError.
reasoning: If the config file is missing the 'num_runs' key, the application will crash with a KeyError.
suggested_fix: Use config.get('num_runs', DEFAULT_VALUE) to provide a fallback or raise a descriptive error message.

title: Subprocess execution failure handling
description: The subprocess call might raise a CalledProcessError, which terminates the worker, but the main process may not report the specific failure well.
reasoning: If the child process crashes, the main process waits for joins, but worker-specific failure reporting could be improved for debugging.
suggested_fix: Wrap the subprocess call in a try-except block to log specific failure details for the worker before re-raising or exiting.

title: Potential IndexOutOfBoundsException in index retrieval
description: The logic assumes that the matrices are perfectly structured as defined in the request, but if a row is empty, indices might fail.
code_snippet: need = [[maximum[i][j] - allocation[i][j] for j in range(r)] for i in range(p)]
reasoning: Though validation is performed earlier, the logic relies on nested indexing that is sensitive to the dimensions 'r' and 'p'.
suggested_fix: Add boundary checks or ensure validation strictly enforces the 'r' column width for every single row.

title: Improper logging configuration
description: The logger attempts to use a format string that expects a custom 'worker_id' field in every log call.
reasoning: Standard log calls (e.g., logger.info(msg)) will fail because they lack the 'worker_id' field in the 'extra' dict, leading to log errors.
suggested_fix: Change the log format to not require 'worker_id' by default or ensure all logging calls provide the 'extra' dictionary.

title: Redundant deep copy of matrix
description: The logic performs deep copies of the matrices using list comprehensions, but in many cases, this is not needed if the logic only reads the data.
reasoning: The allocation of memory for deep copies of matrices in every request can impact performance under high load.
suggested_fix: Only copy matrices when modification is actually required, or use shallow copies if the data structure allows.
